summary: Import-time execution of AES-encrypted hidden payload
details: Requiring or importing aes-decode-runner-pro@1.0.3 immediately executes attacker-controlled JavaScript hidden inside an encrypted blob. The top-level entry point index.js line 3 calls pkg.run() unconditionally, which resolves through runDefaultDecodedFunction → finalFinalDecodeAndRun and decrypts DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT (in src/config/defaults.js) using a hardcoded AES-256-GCM passphrase ("default-dev-passphrase") and salt ("encode-npm-c-salt"), then passes the decrypted string to `new Function(String(decoded.decodedPlainText))()` at src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js:53. The payload is wrapped in three encoding layers (position-unit-codec, encode-decode-codec, AES-256-GCM) supplied by sibling dependencies that together form a self-contained decoder toolchain; the plaintext code never appears in the package source and cannot be audited. Package metadata (`author: ""`, no repository, no keywords) is placeholder, consistent with a throwaway dropper. Because the key and salt are embedded, the payload runs on every machine that imports this package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
